project: Ink CLI Terminal Rendering Parity — Web-Derived Completion
description: Complete the Web Compact Terminal reference matrix and add shared fixtures that expose every known layout, behavior, command, JSON, markdown, details, status, and room/session naming difference before implementation continues.

userStories.title: Create Web-vs-Ink terminal difference matrix
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a complete Web-vs-Ink matrix so that parity gaps are explicit and owned.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a matrix document under the change docs that lists Web law, current Ink behavior, target Ink behavior, source files, fixture coverage, and owning PRD/story for every required area
Matrix includes layout/header, rows, spacing, preview/details, JSON, markdown, status, streaming, slash commands, pickers, room/session naming, no-color, and narrow terminal behavior
Matrix references `web-terminal-reference-audit.md` and `TERMINAL_DESIGN.md` for every major rule

Web UI preservation gate passes: Web Compact Terminal visual/behavioral render logic remains unchanged unless the story explicitly documents user-approved Web behavior changes; shared `src/session-ui/**` changes include Web regression evidence.
Implementation does not import Web DOM/Tailwind/lucide components into CLI modules
Secrets are redacted in previews, details, fixtures, snapshots, and PTY artifacts
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Added docs/specs/changes/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/web-terminal-difference-matrix.md with Web law, current Ink gap, target behavior, fixture coverage, owner PRD/story, and validation gate rows for header, row grammar, spacing, preview/details, JSON, markdown/code, status, streaming, slash commands, pickers, room/session naming, no-color/narrow, and redaction. Evidence tier: docs/source plus focused fixture tests; no PTY artifact required for this matrix-only story. Web UI preservation: no Web Compact Terminal visual/behavioral files changed; no src/session-ui production changes. Validation: docker worker npm run build; node --test test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; npm run typecheck.

userStories.title: Add renderer-neutral long-output and detail fixtures
userStories.description: As an implementer, I want long-output fixtures so preview and expansion rules can be tested without live provider calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Add shared fixtures with at least 12 output lines for tool.call, tool.result-like rows, yielded.run, async agent, shell command, and execution.command
Fixtures retain full input/output/error payloads while marking the collapsed preview expectation as five lines
Fixture tests assert no per-line character truncation in visible preview lines

Web UI preservation gate passes: Web Compact Terminal visual/behavioral render logic remains unchanged unless the story explicitly documents user-approved Web behavior changes; shared `src/session-ui/**` changes include Web regression evidence.
Implementation does not import Web DOM/Tailwind/lucide components into CLI modules
Secrets are redacted in previews, details, fixtures, snapshots, and PTY artifacts
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Added renderer-neutral web-derived long-output fixture helpers in test/fixtures/terminal-parity-fixtures.mjs with 12-line outputs for tool.call, tool.result-like rows, agent.async, yielded.run, shell-command tool, and execution.command. Focused tests assert first five preview lines, +7 more-lines affordance, no character truncation marker, bounded collapsed text, expandable rows, and full line 12 retained in output for details. Evidence tier: shared fixture/source/unit, fake deterministic fixture (not live provider); no PTY required in PRD08. Validation: npm run build; node --test test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; npm run typecheck.

userStories.title: Add JSON, markdown, and syntax fixtures
userStories.description: As an implementer, I want JSON and markdown fixtures so syntax/render semantics are not inferred ad hoc.
acceptanceCriteria: Add nested JSON fixture with object keys, arrays, string, number, boolean, null, long string, circular-safe or prepared equivalent, and secret-like values
Add markdown fixture with headings, paragraphs, nested lists, blockquote, inline code, fenced bash/json code, table, and links
Fixture tests assert renderer-neutral token roles or expected semantic markers for JSON and markdown outputs

Web UI preservation gate passes: Web Compact Terminal visual/behavioral render logic remains unchanged unless the story explicitly documents user-approved Web behavior changes; shared `src/session-ui/**` changes include Web regression evidence.
Implementation does not import Web DOM/Tailwind/lucide components into CLI modules
Secrets are redacted in previews, details, fixtures, snapshots, and PTY artifacts
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Added nestedJsonSyntaxFixture and markdownSyntaxFixture covering function-call JSON roles, nested objects/arrays, strings/numbers/booleans/null, >140-char string, JSON-looking detail text, secret-bearing source value for future redaction assertions, headings, paragraph, quote, nested list, table, inline code, fenced bash, and fenced JSON. Evidence tier: shared fixture/source/unit; fixture includes source secret only for redaction-path tests and no renderer snapshot/log exposes it. Validation: npm run build; node --test test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; npm run typecheck.

userStories.title: Add room/session naming and slash-command fixtures
userStories.description: As an SSH operator, I want fixtures that prove the TUI shows Web room names and command behavior, not only IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fixtures for default room, renamed room, archived room, missing room fallback, session with title, and session with only ID
Add slash command fixture cases for every command in `CLI_ONLY_SLASH_COMMANDS` and `WEB_PARITY_SLASH_COMMANDS`
Tests assert command catalog grouping, command labels, room labels, session labels, and fallback behavior are stable

Web UI preservation gate passes: Web Compact Terminal visual/behavioral render logic remains unchanged unless the story explicitly documents user-approved Web behavior changes; shared `src/session-ui/**` changes include Web regression evidence.
Implementation does not import Web DOM/Tailwind/lucide components into CLI modules
Secrets are redacted in previews, details, fixtures, snapshots, and PTY artifacts
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Added roomSessionNamingFixture with named/default/archived/missing-title rooms and titled/missing-room sessions, plus slashCommandBehaviorFixture derived from CLI_ONLY_SLASH_COMMANDS and WEB_PARITY_SLASH_COMMANDS with dynamic/browser-only fixture cases. Tests assert all catalog commands are represented and room/session primary labels prefer Web names with IDs as secondary/fallback metadata. Evidence tier: shared fixture/source/unit, deterministic fixture not live store. Validation: npm run build; node --test test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; npm run typecheck.

userStories.title: Wire matrix rows to validation ownership
userStories.description: As a Ralph runner, I want every discovered gap to map to a future story so work can be executed in order.
acceptanceCriteria: Every matrix row has owner PRD/story and validation type: shared test, Web render test, Ink snapshot, PTY, or manual review
README and task docs link the matrix and explain how new differences must be added
No uncovered matrix row may be marked complete without a validation owner

Web UI preservation gate passes: Web Compact Terminal visual/behavioral render logic remains unchanged unless the story explicitly documents user-approved Web behavior changes; shared `src/session-ui/**` changes include Web regression evidence.
Implementation does not import Web DOM/Tailwind/lucide components into CLI modules
Secrets are redacted in previews, details, fixtures, snapshots, and PTY artifacts
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Wired each matrix row to fixture id, owner PRD/story, and validation gate; README and tasks now link the matrix and require newly discovered differences to be added with fixture coverage and owner before completion. Evidence tier: docs/source plus WEB_DERIVED_TERMINAL_MATRIX_COVERAGE unit assertions. Web UI preservation: no direct Web behavior changes and no shared production model changes. Validation: npm run build; node --test test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs; npm run typecheck.
